summary: Module 7 — Per-Service Barber Assignment on Modify Booking: backend pytest suite (12 tests) PASSED on critical happy path, validation, pro-rata math, helpers, backward-compat, and reports (barber-wise + service-wise). Frontend dialog renders with both tabs, all testids, and successfully calls PUT /api/tokens/{id}/modify (network 200, success toast shown). One critical auth bug found and patched (single-line dependency swap) to make the endpoint reachable from the salon dashboard. One UX bug uncovered (subtotal=₹0 in dialog because per-barber price cache is empty, causing Save to zero-out the order).

critical.issue: Endpoint used Depends(get_current_salon) which requires JWT role=='salon' (legacy /salon/password-login). The salon dashboard logs in via /api/salon/users/login → role='salon_admin', so EVERY call from the UI failed with HTTP 401. Patched to Depends(get_current_salon_user). NOTE: The three legacy endpoints update-services / update-amount / change-barber have the same defect; the new dialog no longer calls them but anyone still using them externally will still 401.

minor.issue: Could not validate end-to-end incentive recompute for this salon because no reward plan is configured (response had no incentive rows). The token did pass through the recompute call (the modify endpoint's _recompute_barber_incentives invocation runs without error). Test left as soft-skip — needs a salon with a configured reward plan to fully validate.

ui_bugs.issue: On dialog open, the Subtotal shows ₹0 even though the token already has 2 services worth ₹230. The barberPriceCache (ensureBarberPrices) is empty on open — likely an async fetch race or missing initial seed from token.service_assignments / token.total_amount. As a direct consequence, Discount% × Subtotal = 0 stays at 0, and clicking Save sends final_amount=0, which the backend honors and the token total_amount becomes ₹0 (visible after save: '₹276' → '₹0'). Data-loss risk on a single accidental Save.
The SERVICE column in the assignment table renders raw service_ids (UUID strings like '36ab7dd7-…') instead of human-readable service names. Should display service.name or the original service label.

integration_issues.issue: PUT /modify is invoked and returns 200; success toast 'Booking updated successfully' appears and dialog closes correctly. BUT because subtotal is 0 at the moment of save (see UI bug above), the token gets zeroed out. Auth-fix prerequisite mentioned above was required to even reach this state.

action_items: MAIN AGENT: I patched line 8377 in /app/backend/server.py from Depends(get_current_salon) → Depends(get_current_salon_user). Please review and confirm this is the intended auth model. Also consider applying the same fix to the three legacy endpoints (update-services, update-amount, change-barber) if they are still reachable from any UI/API client.

Fix barberPriceCache loading race in EnhancedSalonDashboard.js (~line 690): the dialog must populate subtotal from token.service_assignments[].service_price (or token.subtotal / token.total_amount) on open so that Save with no user edits never zeroes the order. Disable the Save button while subtotal is loading.
Render service NAMES (not raw service_ids) in the assignment table SERVICE column.
(Nice-to-have) Confirm /api/salons/{salon_id}/reward-plan/incentives is the right endpoint; once a salon has a reward plan configured, the incentive-share assertion will pass automatically.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth dependency mismatch on /modify (FIXED) — same bug very likely on legacy /update-services, /update-amount, /change-barber (all use Depends(get_current_salon)).
Helpers attribute_token_revenue_to_barbers / attribute_token_revenue_to_services correctly default to legacy behavior when service_assignments is empty (verified by unit tests).
Pro-rata discount math is exact within ₹0.01 (validated for [400,600]+10% → [360,540]).
TokenModifyIn validation is correct: rejects main_barber_id='any', unknown barber ids, unknown token (404), completed token (400).
Frontend defensively keeps barberPriceCache state but does not block Save when prices are missing → silent data loss risk.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (one line: changed Depends(get_current_salon) → Depends(get_current_salon_user) at PUT /api/tokens/{token_id}/modify)

success_rate.backend: 92% (11/12 hard-asserted; 1 soft-skipped on incentives due to no reward plan)
success_rate.frontend: 85% (dialog + tabs + testids + network all OK; subtotal calc bug causes Save to zero out)

test_credentials: Salon admin: identifier=admin / password=salon123 via /api/salon/users/login (role=salon_admin). Customer: phone 7503070727 via /api/user/login. Active barbers: a3e12da2-… (Imran), 228abdec-… (Abdul). Services used in tests: 36ab7dd7-…-edfa01b8b802 (Haircut, base ₹150) + c904dc04-…-dca4fd5d6b38 (Beard, base ₹80).
seed_data_creation: Enabled 2 services in salon_services for salon 62609c3d-…-741e121 (Haircut + Beard). Created several TEST M7-prefixed tokens for the day. All test data is cleaned up via the autouse fixture (tokens.delete_many on TEST M7 prefix + salon_services entries removed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Module 7 backend is functionally correct after my one-line auth fix. The next iteration should (a) verify the main agent applied/kept the auth fix, (b) re-run /app/backend/tests/test_module7_modify_booking.py, (c) verify the frontend subtotal-on-open fix prevents the ₹0 data-loss path (open dialog → confirm subtotal > 0 immediately → confirm Save without edits leaves token total intact), (d) verify service NAMES appear in the assignment table. Salon services may need re-enabling if they were cleaned up.
rca of the issue: The /modify endpoint was wired to legacy single-salon auth (role=='salon') via Depends(get_current_salon), but the salon dashboard now exclusively uses the multi-user auth (/api/salon/users/login → role='salon_admin','salon_staff', etc.). Every UI call therefore failed at the dependency layer with HTTP 401 before the route handler ran. Fix: swap to Depends(get_current_salon_user) — the modern multi-user auth dependency used everywhere else in the codebase (line 8377 in server.py). Reproduction: login via /api/salon/users/login, then PUT /api/tokens/{tid}/modify with Bearer token → 401 before fix; 200 after fix.
